source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHAT YOU'LL ACTUALLY DO

If you're obsessed with technology and love grinding on complex systems, this is where you'll showcase your technical skills while solving hands-on operational challenges for law firm owners.

You'll deploy CRM configurations, project management systems, automation workflows, analytics dashboards and AI implementations while identifying business bottlenecks and designing solutions that streamline entire practices.

At Swans, we are assembling a team of "Avengers": technical problem-solvers who walk into struggling firms, identify the exact operational issues killing their growth, then build and deploy technical solutions that unlock millions in revenue potential for our clients.




ABOUT SWANS

 - Our Company: We are a fast-moving consulting & implementation agency that improves the way US law firms use technology to deliver better services to their clients.

 - Our Team: We're currently an international team of 10 and expect to grow to 30+ in the next 12 months.

 - Our Culture: We are energetic, no-BS, intense, results-oriented, and driven to change our industry forever.




DAY IN THE LIFE

The role has a dual nature:

 - 75% hands-on system design, building & implementation

 - 25% operational analysis, consulting & client interaction

The technical side of the job will have you solve challenges across 5 main areas:

 - CRM systems: A firm’s slow intake team is losing viable cases. You will build the automated email, SMS & AI workflows that ensure instant lead response and qualification.

 - Case Management / Project Management systems: Staff have abandoned their system, leaving hundreds of overdue tasks. You will standardize, automate, and clear this backlog, making the system a reliable single source of truth.

 - Data & Analytics: A firm owner is "gambling" on marketing, lacking clear financial data. You will build the data pipeline and profitability dashboard that provides real-time clarity.

 - Automation: Staff waste hours manually copying data into 200+ confusing document templates. You will consolidate these into smart templates and build an automation that generates complex documents perfectly in seconds.

 - AI: A firm is overwhelmed by repetitive tasks and suffers from low tech adoption. You will lead AI training sessions that embeds practical, safe, and efficient AI use into their daily work.

We are tool-agnostic and believe in using the best solution for the problem. You will gain deep expertise in a variety of best-in-class platforms (both generic and legal-specific).

The client side includes challenges like:

 - Uncovering law firm operational challenges and connecting these to technical solutions

 - Autonomously gathering requirements, presenting to & training client teams, and managing adoption of deployed solutions

 - Building working relationships with clients and ensuring their high satisfaction & NPS

You will be handling anywhere from 1 to 4 client engagements at a time (depending on their size & complexity), owning the full implementation while being supported by a senior consultant and an extensive knowledge base of past client projects & solutions. Our end-to-end client engagements usually have a duration of at least 6 months, so you will get a chance to truly dig in and get familiar with the clients' teams & processes to drive meaningful impact.




WHY THIS CHANGES EVERYTHING FOR YOU

If you're a high-performer frustrated by corporate bureaucracy, endless approvals, and career ladders based on tenure, this is your off-ramp. We are an anti-bureaucratic team for ambitious professionals who want their impact measured by results, not by time-in-seat.

Conversely, if you're a high-impact freelancer tired of the "feast-or-famine" cycle and juggling the overhead of sales & admin, this is your place. We provide a steady stream of complex, high-value clients so you can focus 100% on building solutions. You get to maintain the high ownership you love, but with the stability an

source_jobs.title: Salesforce Consultant
source_jobs.description:
Overview

If you love getting into the weeds of Salesforce and making it actually work for the people using it, this is your role. Hands-on configuration, end-to-end system ownership and real deployment that transforms how our clients run their sales and operations.

At Swans, we are assembling a team of "Avengers": implementation specialists who walk into organizations, identify the exact system gaps holding them back, and build solutions that unlock growth.

About Swans

 - Our Company: We are a fast-moving consulting & implementation agency that improves the way US law firms use technology to deliver better services to their clients.

 - Our Team: We're currently an international team of 10 and expect to grow to 30+ in the next 12 months.

 - Our Culture: We are energetic, no-BS, intense, results-oriented, and driven to change our industry forever.

What you'll actually do

Our clients have Salesforce - or need it. Your job is to configure, deploy, and optimize it so it actually gets used and actually drives results. You will own implementations end-to-end: from scoping and design through build, testing, and adoption.

You will work across the core pillars of a Salesforce deployment - custom objects, workflows, automation, reporting, integrations, and user setup - and deliver a system that fits the client's operations, not a generic out-of-the-box setup. You will be the person who gets in, figures out what needs to be built, builds it, and makes sure it sticks.

Day in the life

You are embedded in multiple client engagements at a time. You'll gather requirements, design the configuration, build it out, and work directly with client teams to drive adoption. Each engagement is different, but the through-line is always the same: take a messy, underutilized CRM and turn it into the operational backbone of the business.

You are not a Salesforce trainer or a project manager. You are the person who actually builds the thing.

Why this changes everything for you

If you are a high-performer frustrated by corporate bureaucracy, endless approvals, and career ladders based on tenure, this is your off-ramp. We are an anti-bureaucratic team for ambitious professionals who want their impact measured by results, not by time-in-seat.

If you have been doing Salesforce implementations and love the craft but are tired of bloated teams, slow timelines, and projects that never quite ship - this is your place. You get full ownership of your implementations, a lean team behind you, and clients who will actually feel the difference you make.

What we need

 - Hands-on Salesforce configuration experience - You have personally built and deployed Salesforce environments. You know the platform deeply and can work autonomously across its core features.

 - Implementation mindset - You design for adoption. You do not just configure what the client asks for - you understand the business and build what they actually need.

 - Builder mentality - You take ownership of your projects from scoping to go-live. You do not wait for someone else to unblock you.

 - Autonomy - You can gather requirements, make smart decisions, and deliver without being micromanaged.

 - Curiosity - You stay current on Salesforce updates and adjacent tools and apply new capabilities practically.

 - Communication skills - You can explain what you are building and why to non-technical stakeholders. Client-facing experience is a plus, not a requirement.

 - Professional English fluency - B2 level at a minimum.

Ideal background

 - 2-5 years of hands-on Salesforce configuration and implementation experience

 - Background in any industry is welcome. Experience in professional services, consulting, or agencies is a strong plus, as is prior client-facing experience

 - Ideally configured Salesforce deployments in companies of 50-200 people where you owned your projects end-to-end

 - Salesforce Administrator or Consultant certification preferred

 - Experience configuring: custom ob

source_jobs.title: Applied AI Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Overview

If you are obsessed with AI and love turning cutting-edge tools into real, deployed systems that people actually use, this is your role. Not research, not demos - implementation. You will be the person who brings AI into our clients' organizations and makes it stick.

At Swans, we are assembling a team of "Avengers": technical problem-solvers who walk into organizations, identify where AI can unlock the most value, and build and deploy the systems that make it happen.

About Swans

 - Our Company: We are a fast-moving consulting & implementation agency that improves the way US law firms use technology to deliver better services to their clients.

 - Our Team: We're currently an international team of 10 and expect to grow to 30+ in the next 12 months.

 - Our Culture: We are energetic, no-BS, intense, results-oriented, and driven to change our industry forever.

What you'll actually do

AI adoption is the single biggest unlock for the organizations we work with - and the gap between "we have access to AI tools" and "AI is embedded in how we work" is enormous. Your job is to close that gap.

You will deploy AI solutions that are practical, safe, and built to last. This means working across the full stack of modern AI implementation: custom integrations, automation workflows, LLM-powered tools, and hands-on enablement with client teams. You will build the connectors, agents, and workflows that make AI actually work inside our clients' existing systems - and you will help our internal team move faster with AI as well.

Your primary toolkit will be the Claude and Google ecosystems, and you will stay at the cutting edge of what is possible with both.

Day in the life

You are embedded in client engagements and internal projects simultaneously. On the client side, you are building custom AI integrations, deploying workflows, and running enablement sessions that drive real adoption. Internally, you are the person who helps the team move faster by identifying and deploying new AI capabilities as they emerge.

No two weeks look the same. One week you are building a custom MCP connector to pipe client data into an AI workflow. The next you are deploying a Make automation that replaces hours of manual work. The week after that you are running a training session that changes how a team of 10 people does their job.

You are not a researcher or a prompt engineer. You build things, deploy them, and make sure they work.

Why this changes everything for you

If you are a high-performer frustrated by corporate bureaucracy, endless approvals, and career ladders based on tenure, this is your off-ramp. We are an anti-bureaucratic team for ambitious professionals who want their impact measured by results, not by time-in-seat.

If you are someone who has been doing AI work and loves the pace of the field but wants to actually see your work deployed and making a difference - rather than sitting in a research pipeline - this is your place. You will ship constantly, learn constantly, and own your outcomes.

What we need

 - Applied AI expertise - You are deeply familiar with the current AI landscape and know how to deploy it practically. You follow what is happening with LLMs closely and apply new capabilities fast.

 - Builder mentality - You do not just advise. You build the integrations, configure the tools, and deploy the systems yourself.

 - Technical breadth - You are comfortable working across APIs, automation platforms, and AI frameworks without needing a dedicated engineer to support you.

 - Autonomy - You can identify what needs to be built, figure out how to build it, and deliver without being micromanaged.

 - Enablement mindset - You genuinely enjoy helping others use AI effectively and can translate complex capabilities into practical habits for non-technical teams.

 - Curiosity - The field moves fast. You move with it.

 - Communication skills - You can explain AI concepts clearly to non-technical stakeholders. Client-facing

source_jobs.title: Business Intelligence Analyst
source_jobs.description:
OVERVIEW

If you live and breathe data and love building clarity out of chaos, this is your role. You won't be one cog in a 15-person data team, you'll own the full data lifecycle end-to-end, from raw extraction to compelling, insightful dashboards that drive key decisions for our clients.

At Swans, we are assembling a team of "Avengers": analytical problem-solvers who walk into organizations, uncover the data blind spots killing their growth, then design and deploy the systems that turn guesswork into a competitive edge.

About Swans

 - Our Company: We are a fast-moving consulting & implementation agency that improves the way US law firms use technology to deliver better services to their clients.

 - Our Team: We're currently an international team of 10 and expect to grow to 30+ in the next 12 months.

 - Our Culture: We are energetic, no-BS, intense, results-oriented, and driven to change our industry forever.

What you'll actually do

Our clients are making high-stakes decisions in the dark. You will change that. Your job is to take fragmented, messy data, spread across disconnected tools and systems, and turn it into a single source of operational truth.

You will own the full data lifecycle: from extraction and cleaning, through transformation and modeling, to compelling dashboards that surface the insights leaders actually act on. You won't hand off steps to other people, you'll own the pipeline start to finish and deliver clarity that drives real decisions.

You'll also layer in automation and AI where they make the data smarter, fresher, and less dependent on manual effort. If there's a better way to do it, you'll find it.


DAY IN THE LIFE

You're embedded in multiple client engagements at a time, owning the data function end-to-end. You'll identify what data matters, figure out how to get it, clean and structure it, and deliver dashboards and insights that give decision-makers a clear picture of what's happening in their business and what to do about it.

You're not just a report-builder. You're a strategic data partner who can take a business problem, trace it back to the data, and come back with something that changes how leadership operates.

Why this changes everything for you

If you're a high-performer frustrated by corporate bureaucracy, endless approvals, and career ladders based on tenure, this is your off-ramp. We are an anti-bureaucratic team for ambitious professionals who want their impact measured by results, not by time-in-seat.

If you've been the "only data person" at a company and loved the ownership but lacked the opportunity - this is your upgrade. You get the same end-to-end ownership, plus an elite team backing you up.

What we need

 - Full data lifecycle ownership: You have personally owned the complete pipeline: extraction, cleaning, transformation, modeling, and visualization. You haven't specialized in just one layer.

 - Builder mentality: You don't wait for a data engineer to set up the pipeline. You build it, own it, and fix it.

 - Analytical clarity: You don't just surface numbers; you translate data into insights that non-technical stakeholders can act on. You ask the right questions before you write a single query.

 - Autonomy: You can identify what data is needed, figure out how to get it, and deliver the answer without being micromanaged.

 - Curiosity: You stay sharp on new tools, automation approaches, and AI capabilities and apply them practically.

 - Communication skills: You can present findings clearly to a non-technical audience. Client-facing experience is a plus, not a requirement.

 - Professional English fluency: B2 level at a minimum.

Ideal background

 - 2–5 years of experience in a BI, data analyst, or analytics engineering role

 - Background in any industry is welcome we don't require domain expertise. That said, experience in professional services, consulting, or agencies (rather than purely product/SaaS) is a strong plus, as is prior client-facing experience

source_jobs.title: Stakeholder Engagement Manager
source_jobs.description:
WHAT YOU'LL ACTUALLY DO

If you're obsessed with high-stakes client relationships, thrive under pressure, and know how to turn complex technical work into outcomes executives actually care about, this is your role.

You will own the client relationship on Swans engagements end-to-end. That means walking into law firms, earning the trust of founders and senior leadership, translating what our Legal Engineers build into undeniable business value, and making sure every engagement drives results that speak for themselves.



At Swans, we are assembling a team of "Avengers": a rare mix of technically fluent operators and elite relationship owners who together transform how US law firms use technology. You are the latter. You are the person in the room when things get tense, when a partner questions the investment, or when the team is resistant to change. You do not flinch. You move things forward.




ABOUT SWANS

Our Company: We are the operational architect for the fastest growing personal injury firms in the US.

Our Team: We are currently an international team, and we plan to grow our core foundational team deliberately. This role is part of that foundation, contributing to building a robust culture with no ceiling for future opportunities as we scale.

Our Culture: We are energetic, no-BS, intense, results-oriented, and driven to change our industry forever.




DAY IN THE LIFE

The role has a dual nature:

60% client relationship ownership and communication You are the primary point of contact for law firm leadership across active engagements. You run the executive meetings, manage expectations, surface risks early, and make sure the client always knows what was delivered and why it matters.

40% project oversight and cross-functional coordination You work closely with Legal Engineers to understand what is being built, translate deliverables into client-facing language, and ensure every sprint ends with outcomes the client can see and feel.

The client side of the job will have you solve challenges like:

Stakeholder trust: A managing partner is skeptical about the investment after a slow first month. You diagnose the concern, address it directly with data and context, and rebuild confidence without overpromising.

Change management: Staff are resistant to a new system the Legal Engineer has built. You design the adoption approach, run the training sessions, and own the outcome.

Escalation handling: Something breaks, timelines shift, or expectations are misaligned. You are the first call, not the last resort.

Value communication: A firm owner does not see the connection between what was built and what it means for their revenue. You make that connection unmistakably clear.

You will manage anywhere from 2 to 5 active client engagements at a time, owning the relationship layer while being tightly paired with a Legal Engineer who owns the technical layer. Our end-to-end engagements typically run at least 6 months, so you will develop deep knowledge of each client's team, culture, and operational challenges.




WHY THIS CHANGES EVERYTHING FOR YOU

If you are a high-performing account manager or consultant frustrated by bureaucracy, low-value clients, and deals that do not move the needle, this is your off-ramp.

You will be working on transformational projects with law firms doing tens of millions in revenue, sitting across the table from founders and C-suite executives, and owning outcomes that are directly tied to your compensation.

Conversely, if you are an ambitious operator tired of maintaining relationships rather than driving them, this is your place. We do not want account managers who check in. We want people who push, challenge, and deliver.




WHAT WE NEED

Elite executive communication: You have sat across from founders, managing partners, and senior executives and held your own. You know how to deliver difficult news, handle objections without capitulating, and command a room without overpowering it.

Technical

notes: Swans is a fast-moving consulting & implementation agency that improves the way US law firms use technology. Most job postings are technical roles.
